mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Defender TD5 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Defender TD5 engine typically costs £2,800–£4,200 supply only. Fitting adds around £1,000–£1,700 depending on location and workshop rates. A fully fitted replacement from a specialist typically costs approximately £5,980 including VAT. Used TD5 engines start from £800–£1,400.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Defender TD5 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The TD5 is widely regarded as one of Land Rover's most durable diesel engines — with proper maintenance, 300,000+ miles is achievable. However, the 10P (pre-2002) and 15P (post-2002) engines have different risk profiles. The 15P introduced metal head dowels and addressed the oil pump bolt issue, making it the more reliable choice.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Defender TD5?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Oil pump bolt loosening on 10P engines — can seize the engine, (2) Oil contamination in the injector loom — causes misfires and rough running, (3) Cylinder head cracking around injector ports — more common on 10P engines with plastic dowels.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Defender TD5 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £8,000–£15,000 Defender TD5, a £4,500 recon is 30–55% of the car's value — often worth it given Defender values have remained strong. However, if the chassis or bulkhead is rotten, evaluate restoration costs before committing to an engine replacement.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Defender TD5 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine number is stamped on the left-hand side of the cylinder block (turbo side), about halfway along below the exhaust manifold. It will start with 10P, 15P, or 16P. 10P engines were fitted up to 2001; 15P/16P engines from 2002 onwards.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the TD5 oil pump bolt issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative inspection and thread lock application costs £100–£300 and can prevent catastrophic engine failure. If the oil pressure light comes on, stop driving immediately — towing to a garage for inspection could save your engine.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Defender TD5 Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: TD5 is no longer manufactured new

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the Classic-generation TD5. A fully fitted replacement from a specialist typically costs around £5,980 including VAT.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Defender TD5 engine typically takes 10–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). A refundable exchange surcharge of approximately £600 may apply if the old engine cannot be reconditioned.

cta.label: → Compare Defender TD5 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Oil Pump Bolt Failure (10P engines, pre-2002)
problems.symptoms: Low oil pressure warning light, increased engine noise, knocking, sudden engine seizure
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — critical failure can occur without warning

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the oil pressure light comes on, stop driving immediately

problems.recommendation: The oil pump bolt not having thread lock is one of the most serious TD5 issues — it can work loose and seize the engine entirely. This was rectified on later 15P engines. If you own a 10P Defender TD5, have the oil pump bolt inspected and secured with thread lock as a priority. It's a cheap preventative fix that could save you from a £5,000+ engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil pump failure guide

problems.title: Oil Contamination in Injector Loom
problems.symptoms: Misfires, rough idling, power loss, poor engine performance
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)
problems.recommendation: The TD5's injector loom can wick engine oil all the way to the ECU, causing electrical faults and poor running. The injector harness should be treated as a service item. Replacing the rocker cover gasket at the same time is recommended to prevent future oil leaks. If you notice misfires or rough running, check the ECU red connector for oil contamination first.

failureLink.label: Read the full injector loom failure guide

problems.title: Cylinder Head Cracking (10P engines)
problems.symptoms: Engine misfires, oil leaks, diesel in engine oil (dipstick smells dieselly), rising oil level, engine runs on after switching off

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear

problems.recommendation: Early TD5s (10P) used plastic dowels to align the cylinder head, which can fail under heat and allow the head to shift, causing head gasket failure or cracking around the injector ports. The 15P engine introduced metal dowels to fix this. The AMC aftermarket cylinder head is widely considered superior to the original Land Rover item. If you're buying a 10P TD5, factor in a preventative head inspection or budget for an AMC replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full cylinder head failure guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Defender TD5 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — the cheapest fix on this list
Fitting an AMC head is a long-term investment

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Defender TD5? Check These First
buyIf: * 15P or 16P engine (post-2002) with the improved oil pump bolt and metal head dowels
* Documented oil pump bolt inspection or thread lock applied (10P engines)
* Full service history with 5,000-mile oil change intervals
* Recent injector loom replacement with OEM part
avoidIf: * 10P engine with no oil pump bolt history — this is a time bomb
* Cold-start issues, misfires, or rough running with no diagnosis
* Diesel smell on dipstick (suggests injector seal leak or head crack)
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis or fault codes

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The TD5 is one of Land Rover's most beloved diesel engines — a robust 2.5-litre five-cylinder with genuine character and impressive tunability. But the 10P (pre-2002) and 15P (post-2002) engines are different propositions. The 15P fixed the most serious 10P weaknesses — the oil pump bolt and plastic head dowels. Buy a 15P with good history, or budget for the preventative work on a 10P, and you'll have a Defender engine that can comfortably exceed 300,000 miles.

cta.label: → Compare Defender TD5 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Defender TD5 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for knocking, misfires, or rough running
* Oil Pressure Light — Does it illuminate at any point? Stop immediately if it does
* Service History — Including which engine (10P or 15P) and confirmed 5,000-mile oil change intervals
* Dipstick Check — Smell for diesel (injector seal leak or head crack); check for rising oil level
* ECU Connector — Remove the red ECU plug and check for oil contamination
* Smoke Check — Excessive white or black smoke on startup or under load
* Fuel Pressure Regulator — Check for leaks at the rear of the engine
* Cooling System — TD5s are sensitive to overheating; check coolant condition and level
